About
Talk to an engineer

Your canned motor pumps are failing. And you can't see it coming.

They're sealed, inaccessible, and critical. Vibration sensors can't reach them. By the time you know something's wrong, it's already a containment risk. You need visibility — without opening the casing.

Talk to an engineer Read the technical note

The problem isn't the pump. It's the blind spot.

You're responsible for asset reliability. But CMPs make that nearly impossible.

You can't instrument them

The motor and impeller share a sealed casing. There's nowhere to mount a vibration sensor. Opening it defeats the purpose of containment.

Failure is high-consequence

When the rotor contacts the stator can, you risk containment loss. By the time traditional monitoring detects a problem, the damage is done.

Access is restricted or impossible

CMPs operate in ATEX zones, submerged tanks, and confined spaces. Physical inspection is expensive, risky, or simply not an option.

We built SAM4 for exactly this problem.

We've spent years working with reliability engineers who face this same challenge. Assets they're accountable for, but can't physically reach.

SAM4 uses Electrical Signature Analysis to monitor canned motor pumps from the motor control cabinet. No sensors on the pump. No access to the asset. Just continuous visibility from a safe zone.

10,000+
Assets monitored
90.8%
Detection rate (core assets)
80+
Customers
20 min
Install per asset

Three steps to continuous CMP monitoring

Motor returns to service the same shift. No scaffolding. No confined space entry.

1

Open the MCC panel

Installation happens in the motor control cabinet — a safe, accessible location. Not at the pump.

2

Clip on CTs and VTs

SAM4 hardware clips onto existing wiring. 20 minutes per asset. No process interruption.

3

See what your pumps are doing

Data flows to the SAM4 dashboard via cellular or wired connection. First insights during calibration.

What SAM4 detects on canned motor pumps

Since the motor and impeller share a common shaft, the electrical signature acts as a direct transducer for both motor health and pump load.

Hydraulic faults

Cavitation, dry-run, impeller fouling

Electrical faults

Stator winding shorts, voltage imbalances

Mechanical faults

Unbalance, misalignment

Operational anomalies

Flow deviations, efficiency degradation

SAM4 does not claim bearing wear detection on CMPs. We're transparent about what we can and can't detect.

Two paths forward

The choice is between visibility and hope.

With SAM4

  • Continuous visibility into sealed pump health
  • Early fault detection before containment risk
  • Maintenance planned around evidence, not guesswork
  • No field access, back online same shift, no scaffolding
  • Installed in 20 minutes from the MCC

Without monitoring

  • No visibility until failure occurs
  • Unplanned shutdowns and containment incidents
  • Reactive maintenance on your most critical assets
  • Expensive emergency interventions in ATEX zones
  • The risk sits with you, not with the data
"These pumps were always online with no redundancy. SAM4 gave us the confidence we needed."
John McCrystal, Reliability and Maintenance Specialist, DuPont

Stop guessing. Start monitoring.

If you're running canned motor pumps and want continuous visibility without opening the casing, let's have an engineer-to-engineer conversation.

Talk to an engineer Download the technical note

Want to talk through the data with an engineer? We're happy to.
